plan.original_query: What controls apply to API security?

steps.search_query: API security, including authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limiting, and logging for APIs
steps.purpose: Find controls that require securing APIs through strong authentication and authorization, input validation and sanitization, throttling and rate limiting, secure configuration, and logging/monitoring of API activity.

step.search_query: API security, including authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limiting, and logging for APIs
step.purpose: Find controls that require securing APIs through strong authentication and authorization, input validation and sanitization, throttling and rate limiting, secure configuration, and logging/monitoring of API activity.

documents.title: Service Identification and Authentication
documents.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate [Assignment: system services and applications] before establishing communications with devices, users, or other services or applications.

documents.title: Identity Providers and Authorization Servers
documents.description: Employ identity providers and authorization servers to manage user, device, and non-person entity (NPE) identities, attributes, and access rights supporting authentication and authorization decisions in accordance with [Assignment: policy] using [Assignment: mechanisms].

documents.title: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
documents.description: The source and integrity of identity assertions and access tokens are verified before granting access to system and information resources.

documents.title: Limits on Authorized Use
documents.description:
Permit authorized individuals to use an external system to access the system or to process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information only after:
  (a) Verification of the implementation of controls on the external system as specified in the organization’s security and privacy policies and security and privacy plans; or
  (b) Retention of approved system connection or processing agreements with the organizational entity hosting the external system.

documents.title: Public Key-based Authentication
documents.description:
(a) For public key-based authentication:
  (1) Enforce authorized access to the corresponding private key; and
  (2) Map the authenticated identity to the account of the individual or group; and
(b) When public key infrastructure (PKI) is used:
  (1) Validate certificates by constructing and verifying a certification path to an accepted trust anchor, including checking certificate status information; and
  (2) Implement a local cache of revocation data to support path discovery and validation.

documents.title: Service Identification and Authentication
documents.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate [Assignment: system services and applications] before establishing communications with devices, users, or other services or applications.

documents.title: Identity Providers and Authorization Servers
documents.description: Employ identity providers and authorization servers to manage user, device, and non-person entity (NPE) identities, attributes, and access rights supporting authentication and authorization decisions in accordance with [Assignment: policy] using [Assignment: mechanisms].

documents.title: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
documents.description: The source and integrity of identity assertions and access tokens are verified before granting access to system and information resources.

documents.title: Limits on Authorized Use
documents.description:
Permit authorized individuals to use an external system to access the system or to process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information only after:
  (a) Verification of the implementation of controls on the external system as specified in the organization’s security and privacy policies and security and privacy plans; or
  (b) Retention of approved system connection or processing agreements with the organizational entity hosting the external system.

documents.title: Public Key-based Authentication
documents.description:
(a) For public key-based authentication:
  (1) Enforce authorized access to the corresponding private key; and
  (2) Map the authenticated identity to the account of the individual or group; and
(b) When public key infrastructure (PKI) is used:
  (1) Validate certificates by constructing and verifying a certification path to an accepted trust anchor, including checking certificate status information; and
  (2) Implement a local cache of revocation data to support path discovery and validation.

response.answer:
Applicable controls for API security from the supplied list:

- Authenticate service-to-service and application connections used by APIs: require unique identification and authentication of system services and applications before establishing communications (applies to API clients and backend services) [ia-9].

- Use centralized identity/authorization infrastructure for API access control: employ identity providers and authorization servers to manage user, device, and non-person entity identities, attributes, and access rights used in authentication and authorization decisions for API calls, per organizational policy and configured mechanisms [ia-13].

- Verify tokens/assertions presented to APIs: verify the source and integrity of identity assertions and access tokens before granting access to system and information resources via the API (e.g., validate signatures, issuer, audience, expiry) [ia-13.2].

- Enforce limits and conditions on external API consumers/hosts: permit external systems to access or process organization-controlled information only after verifying required controls on the external system or retaining approved connection/processing agreements (useful for third-party APIs and integrations) [ac-20.1].

- Where APIs use public key-based authentication (mutual TLS, client certs, signed requests), protect private keys, map authenticated identities to accounts, validate certificates to a trust anchor including revocation checking, and locally cache revocation data to support validation [ia-5.2].

If you want, I can map these controls to concrete API security measures (e.g., token validation steps, mutual TLS configuration, third-party onboarding checklist) — say which you prefer.

plan.original_query: What policies relate to logging and monitoring?

steps.search_query: logging and monitoring of system and user activity
steps.purpose: Find NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls that require or describe logging, audit records, continuous monitoring, and review of system and user activity.

step.search_query: logging and monitoring of system and user activity
step.purpose: Find NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls that require or describe logging, audit records, continuous monitoring, and review of system and user activity.

documents.title: System Use Notification
Event Logging
Logging and Review
documents.description:
(a) Log [Assignment: organization-defined audit events] for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions; and
(b) Review the audit records of the maintenance and diagnostic sessions to detect anomalous behavior.

documents.title: Session Audit
documents.description:
a. Provide and implement the capability for [Assignment: users or roles] to [Selection (one or more): record; view; hear; log] the content of a user session under [Assignment: circumstances] ; and
b. Develop, integrate, and use session auditing activities in consultation with legal counsel and in accordance with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines.

documents.title: System Monitoring
System Use Notification
Event Logging
Logging and Review
documents.description:
(a) Log [Assignment: organization-defined audit events] for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions; and
(b) Review the audit records of the maintenance and diagnostic sessions to detect anomalous behavior.

documents.title: Session Audit
documents.description:
a. Provide and implement the capability for [Assignment: users or roles] to [Selection (one or more): record; view; hear; log] the content of a user session under [Assignment: circumstances] ; and
b. Develop, integrate, and use session auditing activities in consultation with legal counsel and in accordance with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines.

documents.title: System Monitoring

response.answer:
Policies relating to logging and monitoring are covered by the supplied controls as follows:

- Event logging: Define what event types the system can log, coordinate logging with other organizational entities, specify which event types will actually be logged and how often, document why those event types suffice for post‑incident investigations, and review/update the selected event types on a defined schedule [au-2].

- Session-level capture: Provide capability for designated users/roles to record, view, hear, or log user session content under defined circumstances, and ensure session auditing is developed and used in consultation with legal counsel and applicable authorities [au-14].

- Maintenance session logging and review: Require logging of organization‑defined audit events for nonlocal maintenance/diagnostic sessions and review those maintenance audit records to detect anomalous behavior [ma-4.1].

- System monitoring for attacks and misuse: Monitor systems to detect attacks and indicators of attacks and unauthorized local/network/remote connections; identify unauthorized use using organization‑defined techniques; deploy monitoring capabilities strategically and ad hoc to collect essential information; analyze detected events/anomalies; adjust monitoring when risk changes; obtain legal opinion on monitoring activities; and provide system monitoring information to designated personnel or roles as needed or at defined frequencies [si-4].

- User notification of monitoring: Display a system use notification/banner prior to granting access that informs users the system is a U.S. Government system, that usage may be monitored/recorded/audited, that unauthorized use is prohibited, and that use indicates consent to monitoring; retain the banner until users acknowledge and take explicit logon actions; and for public systems display appropriate system‑use information and authorized‑use descriptions consistent with privacy accommodations [ac-8].

If you need, I can map these controls into draft policy statements or a checklist for operational implementation.

plan.original_query: What is the capital of France?

steps.purpose: Find a control or guidance that mentions the capital of France (note: NIST SP 800-53 controls will not contain this general geography fact).
